title: Hangin' on the Lake
summary:
Feeling very refreshed and clean, we headed south towards warmer weather and the promise of a lakefront camping spot. While we did find warmer weather, the camping spot was next to where a lake might have once been but is no more. It was still a nice free spot and you could see the lake in the distance so we decided to stay to a few nights. The warmer weather and later sunsets made for a relaxing couple of days where we ran, worked a little, played games, went on little walks and watched the Olympics which we have been able to stream with our Starlink. We did eventually make it down to the lake bushwacking through the dried lake bed and had some excitement when Maya stumbled upon a rattlesnake basking on the road, but luckily she listened to our frantic yelling and left it alone.

For our last night at the lake, we decided to move to one of the paid campgrounds so I could shower since I was about at my 4-5 day limit. Still being early February it was only about half full and we found a spot that actually overlooked the lake, though it was still about 100ft down from the site.

-Helen

photos.caption: After taking a hot tub and showering in Payson, we felt nice and clean, meaning it was time to change the sheets in our van.
Van setup at Indian Point Campground.
Our taco breakfast one morning camped at Indian Point Campground.
A family run near Indian Point Campground.
Colin was obsessed with running on the Forest Service trails around Roosevelt Lake. As such, he took many photos of the views from the road. (F.S. Rd. Pic #1)
Maya playing fetch in Roosevelt Lake.
View from the trails around Cholla Campground.
